title: 10 best herbal teas for itchy ears

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Pot marigold tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce swelling and itching in the ear canal by suppressing inflammation.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial properties of pot marigold tea, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, can help to combat infections that may be causing itchiness and discomfort in the ears.

Soothing and antioxidant property: Pot marigold tea's high concentration of antioxidants and soothing compounds, such as saponins and polyphenols, can help to calm and protect the ear canal from further irritation, reducing itching and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tea's soothing and calming effects can reduce redness and swelling, while its antimicrobial properties help combat infections and irritation that may be causing the itchiness. By promoting a healthy balance of flora in the ear canal, Calendula officinalis tea can provide relief from itchy ears and promote a sense of comfort and well-being. Its natural antihistamine properties also help to reduce itching and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: These compounds may help reduce inflammation and itching in the ears by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Phenolic acids: These compounds, particularly triterpenoids and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe and protect the ear canal from infections and inflammation.

Carotenoids: These pigments may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the ear canal by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used because they contain anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and calm itchy ear infections.
Flowers: They are used because they possess antiseptic and antimicrobial properties that aid in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections in the ears.
Seeds: They are used because they contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help to reduce swelling and combat infection in itchy ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the early morning when they are at their peak potency.
Rinse the flowers gently in cold water to remove any dirt or debris that may be present.
Combine the cleaned calendula flowers with 1 cup of boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh to remove the flowers and any sediment.
Store the tea in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and serve chilled or at room temperature.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling and irritation in the ear canal, providing relief from itching and discomfort.

Antibacterial properties: Chamomile's antibacterial compounds, like chamazulene, inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause infection and itchiness in the ears, promoting a healthy ear environment.
Soothing and calming effects: The soothing and calming properties of chamomile tea can help calm irritated skin and reduce itching and discomfort associated with itchy ears.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The tea contains apigenin, an antioxidant that can calm irritated skin and reduce inflammation, providing relief from itchy ears. The antiseptic properties of chamomile tea can also help to prevent infections that may be causing the itchiness. Additionally, its calming effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate ear itching. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for itchy ears.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce itching and inflammation in the ear.
Chamazulene: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antipruritic (anti-itching) properties, which help to soothe itchy ears and reduce inflammation.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce itching and inflammation in the ear, and promote overall ear health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high apigenin and chamazulene content, which provides anti-inflammatory and soothing effects for itchy ears.
Seeds: They contain a higher concentration of apigenin and other beneficial compounds that help to reduce inflammation and itching in the ears.
Leaves: Although less commonly used than flowers, Matricaria chamomilla leaves also possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate itchy ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh flowers of matricaria chamomilla for tea making during peak bloom.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers for every 8 oz of boiling water.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the herbal properties.
Strain the tea and serve immediately or let it cool for later consumption.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and itching in the ears, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantago major, also known as plantain, teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help alleviate itchy ear symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried plantago major leaves and flowers in the amount of 2 teaspoons per 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried herbs in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release their active properties.
Strain the tea mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Add honey or other sweeteners to taste and adjust the flavor of the tea.
Store the cooled tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. remedy_recipe: Gather the required ingredients including 1 cup of taraxacum officinale flowers and 2 cups of boiling water.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried taraxacum officinale flowers for every 8 ounces of water.
Steep the dried taraxacum officinale flowers in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a cup.
Drink the tea immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and itching in the ear canal, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Flowers: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the ear canal, alleviating itching sensations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort leaves and flowers, ensuring they are free from contaminants and pesticides.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, well-ventilated area for at least 24 hours or use a food dehydrator on low heat for several hours.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried hypericum perforatum with 8 ounces of boiling water, steeping for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid for consumption.
Allow the tea to cool and then use it as an ear wash by filling the ear canal with the solution, gently massaging the affected area before draining the excess liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with itchy ears because of its soothing and calming properties. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can reduce itching and discomfort in the ear canal. Additionally, lemon balm's sedative properties can help to relax the muscles in the ear, providing relief from itchiness and irritation caused by allergies or other environmental factors. This makes it a popular natural remedy for relieving itchy ears without harsh chemicals.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon balm tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, help reduce swelling and itching in the ear canal.
Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial compounds in lemon balm tea, like citral and geraniol, inhibit the growth of bacteria, fungi, and viruses that can cause ear infections and itchy ears.
Sedative property: Lemon balm tea's sedative compounds, including volatile oils and flavonoids, help calm irritated nerves and reduce itchiness and discomfort in the ear canal.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lemon balm roots help soothe and calm itchy ear symptoms.
Leaves: Abundant in flavonoids and volatile oils, lemon balm leaves provide a natural analgesic effect to reduce ear discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers for a calming tea blend.
Measure two teaspoons of dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers or one teaspoon of fresh herbs per cup of boiling water.
Steep the herbal mixture in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its soothing properties.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids.
Allow the tea to cool and then use it as an ear drop by dropping 1-2 teaspoons directly into the affected ear.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ironwort teas helps with itchy ears because of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, work together to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes in the ear canal. As a natural antibacterial agent, ironwort tea can also help eliminate underlying infections that may be contributing to itchiness and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infection, ironwort teas provide relief from itchy ears and promote overall ear health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Ironwort tea's antibacterial properties can help combat bacterial infections that cause itchy ears, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Ironwort tea's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe itchy ears by reducing swelling and redness, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
Antiparasitic properties: Ironwort tea's antiparasitic properties can help eliminate parasites such as ear mites or worms that can cause itchiness and irritation in the ears.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm itchiness in the ears.
Barks: The barks of the ironwort plant have been traditionally used to treat ear infections and reduce inflammation, making them a popular choice for relieving itchy ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried sideritis scardica leaves from a trusted source.
Combine the harvested leaves with 250ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature before consumption within an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tea's flavonoids, such as luteolin and apigenin, have been shown to soothe irritated skin and reduce inflammation, providing relief from the discomfort associated with itchy ears. Additionally, lemon verbena's antimicrobial compounds help combat infections that can cause itchiness. By drinking this tea, individuals may experience a calming effect on their ear canal, leading to reduced itching and improved overall ear health.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial compounds present in lemon verbena tea can help combat infections and eliminate bacteria, viruses, or fungi that may be causing ear itchiness and inflammation.
Calming and soothing: Lemon verbena tea has a calming and soothing effect on the skin, which can help ease discomfort and itching associated with itchy ears.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing ear inflammation and itchiness.
Seeds: Containing natural oils that soothe and calm irritated skin, helping to alleviate itching sensations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers according to the recommended dosage of 2-3 teaspoons.
Boil one cup of water in a teapot and let it cool for 5 minutes before adding the herb mixture.
Add the measured quantity of verbena officinalis to the boiling water and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup, discarding any solids.
Enjoy the warm herbal tea within 30 minutes of preparation, drinking 1-2 cups per day as needed for itchy ears relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail teas helps with itchy ears because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tea's high silica content soothes and calms irritated skin, reducing redness and itching. Soothing astringent properties: Horsetail tea's astringent properties can help dry up excessive discharge and reduce itching in the ear canal, while also providing a calming effect on inflamed tissues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried equisetum arvense plants of known identity and quality to ensure efficacy.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried equisetum arvense per cup of boiling water for optimal potency.
Steep the herbal mixture in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release the bioactive compounds.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and ensure clarity.
Allow the cooled tea to infuse for an additional 30 minutes before consumption to enhance absorption.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy ears that are treated with herbal teas are skin irritations, such as eczema and acne. Teas like chamomile, calendula, and peppermint can soothe and calm these conditions by reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Other conditions, such as dandruff and psoriasis, may also be relieved by the anti-inflammatory properties of herbs like lavender and tea tree oil when consumed in a warm herbal tea.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for itchy ears are specialized blends of herbs that help alleviate discomfort and inflammation in the ear canal. They are often used as a natural remedy to soothe itchy ears and provide relief from irritation. The best herbal teas for itchy ears include Calendula officinalis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Echinacea purpurea, Aloe vera, Aloe barbadensis, Lavandula angustifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Achillea millefolium, Ginkgo biloba, and Urtica dioica. If left untreated, itchy ears can lead to sleep disturbances, decreased productivity, and increased stress levels, negatively impacting one's quality of life and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with itchy ears is a blend of calendula, chamomile, and mullein. Calendula's anti-inflammatory properties soothe the skin, while chamomile calms the nerves and reduces irritation. Mullein, rich in antioxidants, helps to combat infections and ease discomfort. This trifecta of teas not only alleviates itching but also promotes overall ear health. To reap the benefits, steep 1 teaspoon of each herb in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and enjoy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tea's active compounds, including triterpenoids and flavonoids, help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Calendula's natural antimicrobial properties also combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause ear itchiness. By promoting a healthy balance of ear flora and reducing irritation, Calendula officinalis teas provide a natural and effective solution for itchy ears, promoting comfort and relief. Regular use may also help prevent future occurrences.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and irritation that may be causing itchy ears.

Flavonoids (specifically quercetin and kaempferol): These antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents can help soothe and calm the skin and mucous membranes in the ear, reducing itchiness and discomfort.

Phenolic acids (specifically caffeic acid and ferulic acid): These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the ear, leading to relief from itchy ears.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm itchy ear infections.
Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce itching and inflammation in the ears.
Seeds: They contain compounds that can help soothe and calm itchy ear infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried calendula officinalis flowers and 1 quart of boiling water to steep for 5 minutes.
Boil 1 quart of water and let it cool slightly for 1 minute before adding the dried flowers.
Mix 1 tablespoon of honey with 1 cup of the cooled calendula tea to sweeten the brew for 2 minutes.
Strain the calendula tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the flowers immediately.
Serve the calendula tea warm or chilled as desired within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia teas helps with itchy ears because of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea, derived from the leaves of the Melaleuca tree, contains compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol, which have been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated skin and ear surfaces. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Melaleuca alternifolia tea can provide relief from itchy ears, promoting a healthy and balanced ear environment. This natural remedy can be a gentle and effective solution for ear discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (eucalyptol): This terpene helps to reduce inflammation and itching by creating a cooling effect on the skin and mucous membranes.
Limonene: As an antimicrobial agent, limonene helps to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause itchiness in the ears.
Methyl cinnamate: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and heal itchy ear infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are a common part used for teas due to their high concentration of oils, particularly cajeput oil, which is known for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: Buds are used in teas because they contain a higher concentration of essential oils than the leaves, providing further relief from itchy ears.
Leaves: (replaced with) Stems: Stems are occasionally used in teas as they contain some essential oils, offering an alternative to leaves for treating itchy ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves from mature melaleuca alternifolia plants early in the morning for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area at a low temperature of 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried leaves per 8 oz of boiling water for the perfect tea.
Steep the dried leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea and serve immediately to enjoy the full benefits of the herbal infusion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas helps with itchy ears because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the ear canal. The tea's natural compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, work to reduce inflammation and combat fungal or bacterial infections that may be causing the itchiness. Additionally, Echinacea's antiseptic qualities help to promote a healthy balance of bacteria in the ear, preventing the growth of pathogens that can lead to itching and discomfort. This natural remedy can provide relief from itchy ears without harsh chemicals.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These constituents have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, which can help reduce itching and inflammation in the ears.
Iridoid Glycosides (Echinacosides): These compounds have immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating itchy ears.

Caffeic Acid Derivatives (Cichoric Acid): These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and protect the ears from further irritation, reducing itching and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe itchy ears.
Leaves: They contain bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and ease discomfort in itchy ears.
Flowers: They are known for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat infections and reduce itching in the ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in the summer months when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a warm place for 1 to 3 weeks or until completely dry.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids before serving.
Store the tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera teas helps with itchy ears because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that can calm the skin and reduce irritation. The anti-inflammatory properties in Aloe vera help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the ear canal, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, Aloe vera's antibacterial properties can help to combat infections that may be causing the itchiness.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and itching associated with itchy ears.
Anthraquinones: Exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate the discomfort and itching associated with itchy ears.
Furanosteroids: Possess anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which may help reduce swelling and itching in the ears.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most used part of Aloe vera for teas due to their high gel content and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots are used in some herbal teas for their potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties to soothe itchy ears.
Stems: Stems are sometimes used to make teas due to their ability to retain the plant's medicinal properties and provide relief from itchy ears.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100ml of aloe vera gel from 1 mature leaf and 1 liter of boiling water.
Combine the aloe vera gel with 100g of dried herbs in a heat-resistant teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and nutrients to infuse.
Strain the tea into a separate container to remove the solids and herbs.
Serve the tea immediately and store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe plant has been used for centuries to calm irritated skin, and when brewed as a tea, it can have a similar effect on the sensitive skin inside the ear. The tea's anti-bacterial and anti-fungal properties also help to eliminate any underlying infections that may be causing the itchiness, promoting a healthy and balanced ear environment. This natural remedy can provide relief and comfort to itchy ears.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: This anthraquinone derivative has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and reduce inflammation in the ear canal, thereby alleviating itchiness.
Aloe-emodin: A phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, aloe-emodin can help combat infections and reduce inflammation that may be causing itchy ears.
Saponins: These triterpene glycosides have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and protect the ear canal from further irritation and infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary part used in Aloe vera teas due to their high content of aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe itchy ears.
Seeds: Aloe vera seeds contain compounds like aloe-emodin and aloin, which can be extracted for use in teas to reduce inflammation and alleviate itchiness in the ears.
Buds: Aloe vera buds, being a rich source of aloe-emodin and other bioactive compounds, are sometimes used in teas to treat itchy ears by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100-150 grams of fresh aloe barbadensis leaves with thick green skin for optimal potency.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with cold water to remove dirt and impurities then drain.
Cut the leaves into small pieces and soak them in 1 liter of cold water for 2 hours.
Steep the soaked aloe barbadensis pieces in 1 liter of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release.
Strain the tea through a fine mesh sieve into a glass container to remove solids and discard.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tea's ability to soothe and calm irritated skin makes it an effective remedy for ear itching caused by infections, allergies, or dryness. The antifungal and antibacterial properties in the tea help to eliminate underlying infections that may be contributing to the itchiness. Additionally, the calming effects of the tea may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate ear itching. This makes it a popular natural remedy for ear itching relief.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as an antihistamine and anti-inflammatory, helping to reduce itching and inflammation in the ears.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, preventing the growth of bacteria and fungi that may cause ear itching and infection.
Caryophyllene: Displays anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, soothing and protecting the skin and mucous membranes in the ears from irritation and infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lavandula angustifolia leaves are used to make teas due to their calming and soothing properties, which can help alleviate itchiness.
Flowers: Lavandula angustifolia flowers are commonly used to make teas as they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe itchy ears.
Stems: Lavandula angustifolia stems are also used to make teas as they contain essential oils that can help calm and reduce inflammation in itchy ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning when they are at their peak potency.
Rinse the flowers with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris that may be present.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers or 2 teaspoons of fresh flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the flowers and any loose particles.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before serving and consuming it immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The tea's active compounds, such as eucalyptol, help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the ear canal. This can provide relief from itchy ears caused by allergies, earwax buildup, or infections. Additionally, the tea's expectorant properties can help to thin and clear out mucus and debris, further reducing itchiness and discomfort in the ears. This can bring much-needed relief to those suffering from itchy ears.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and discomfort in itchy ears.
Eudesmol: This terpene has antimicrobial properties, which help combat infections and eliminate bacteria that may be causing the itchiness.

Volatile oils (including pinene and limonene): These terpenes have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce inflammation and infection in itchy ears, promoting healing and relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used to make teas due to their high eucalyptol content, a natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory compound.
Barks: The barks of the Eucalyptus globulus tree are used to make teas as they contain compounds like eucalyptol, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve itchy ears.
Leaves: Again, the leaves are preferred due to their high eucalyptol content, making them an effective remedy for itchy ears and other respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from the plant or purchase dried leaves from a reputable supplier.
Dry the fresh leaves for 1-2 hours in a low-temperature oven at 150-200 degrees fahrenheit.
Weigh 2-4 teaspoons of dried leaves for a standard serving of herbal tea.
Steep the dried leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea and serve hot or chilled after 10 minutes of preparation time.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium teas helps with itchy ears because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that soothe and calm irritated ear tissues. The tea's active compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones, inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause itchiness. Additionally, its astringent and antipruritic effects help to reduce inflammation and itching sensations, promoting a sense of comfort and relief in the affected area, making it a popular natural remedy for ear itchiness.
remedy_constituents: Chlorogenic Acid: This phenolic acid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort in itchy ears.
Apigenin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes in the ears.
Nepalensetin: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with itchy ears.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe itchy ear symptoms.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, aiding in the relief of itchy ear discomfort.
Roots: Packed with antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate itchy ear symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 25g of dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers from a trusted source.
Steep the dried herb in 250ml of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove solids.
Discard the solids and add honey to taste to sweeten the tea.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before serving.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and redness in the ear canal. The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba also help to soothe and calm the ear, providing relief from itchy sensations. Furthermore, Ginkgo biloba's ability to improve blood flow and oxygenation to the affected area may also contribute to its effectiveness in alleviating ear discomfort. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from ear irritation.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and itching in the ears.
Terpenoids: They possess antiseptic and antibacterial properties, which help combat infections that may be causing the itchiness.
Bilobalide: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and protect the ear tissue from damage and reduce itching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used to make teas due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Seeds are sometimes used in traditional medicine, but their effectiveness in treating itchy ears is not well-documented.
Barks: Barks might be used to make teas, although their use in treating itchy ears is not a common practice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated slowly.
Rinse the leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris from the environment quickly.
Dry the leaves in a single layer on a paper bag for 1-2 weeks or until completely dry.
Steep 1 teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes carefully.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves before serving the herbal tea hot or cold immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica teas helps with itchy ears because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant, also known as stinging nettle, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to soothe skin irritations and relieve itching. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to calm the skin and reduce inflammation, making it a popular natural remedy for itchy ears. Regular consumption of Urtica dioica tea may provide long-lasting relief and promote overall ear health.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, including the ears, thereby alleviating itchy sensations.
Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help calm the skin and reduce itchiness in the ears.

Histamine-releasing inhibitor (HRIs): These compounds, such as polyphenolic flavonoids and terpenoids, can inhibit the release of histamine, a chemical that contributes to itching and inflammation in the ears.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in histamine and other compounds that help to reduce itching and inflammation.
Roots: The roots of Urtica dioica contain a higher concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them a popular choice for teas.
Stems: Stems of Urtica dioica are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, making them effective for itchy ears.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-4 teaspoons of dried urtica dioica leaves and stems in a clean container.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the dried plant material.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active ingredients to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Serve the tea hot or iced and consume within 30 minutes to 1 hour.
